column2: Module Theme (This will be broken into submodules and only submodules name would be there)
column6: Sub module name (Will reflect on learners dashboard)
column7: Why learn this

Mastering data analysis and visualization using NumPy, Pandas, and foundational probability equips you to explore, clean, and interpret data effectively—skills that are essential for any data-driven role in today’s AI-powered world.

Master the two pillars of machine learning—regression and classification. From predicting prices and trends to detecting spam and diagnosing diseases, you’ll learn to build accurate, production-ready models using foundational, ensemble, and scikit-learn pipeline techniques—skills that are essential for real-world ML systems and top interview performance.
column8: Linear Regression, regression metrics and Sklearn pipelines

column6: ML Coding: Unsupervised Learning, Time series & Recommender Systems
column7: Unsupervised learning, time series forecasting, and recommendation systems unlock the ability to uncover hidden patterns, detect anomalies, predict future trends, and deliver personalized experiences. From customer segmentation and fraud detection to sales forecasting and product recommendations, you’ll learn techniques that power real-world applications in finance, e-commerce, and product analytics—giving you a strong edge in both exploratory and AI-driven tasks.

Neural networks form the foundation of modern AI—from image recognition to language models. Learning how to build and optimize them helps you unlock the power of deep learning and prepares you for cutting-edge roles in tech and AI.
column8: Neural Networks, Optimizers, Loss function, activation functions, weight intialization, regularization

column6: MLCoding: Computer Vision and Natural Language Processing
column7: Computer vision and natural language processing (NLP) are transforming how machines perceive and interact with the world—from facial recognition and medical imaging to chatbots, search engines, and large language models like GPT. In this module, you’ll master practical deep learning techniques and tools to build advanced vision systems and intelligent, language-aware applications—empowering you to solve complex, real-world problems at the cutting edge of AI.

column8: Applied Deep Learning (CNNs, LSTM, GRU, Autoencoders, etc)
Advance Computer Vision - Image gen, Face detection / recognition, segmentation
Text Pre-Processing (NLTK/spaCy), topic modelling, Sentiment Analysis

column7: MLOps bridges the gap between model development and real-world deployment—ensuring your ML solutions are scalable, reproducible, and reliable. This module teaches you how to productionize ML with tools like Docker, MLflow, and AWS SageMaker, making you industry-ready for end-to-end AI systems.

column8: ML Monitoring, Experiment tracking, ML flow, Docker, Optuna

column7: Knowing how to build scalable, efficient, and cost-effective ML systems is crucial for delivering real-world AI products. This module equips you with the fundamentals of ML system design, microservices, and architectural patterns—skills highly valued in top tech roles.

column6: Foundations of AI Engineering & RAG Systems
column7: Moving beyond basic APIs, this module teaches you to build robust, hallucination-free AI apps grounded in proprietary enterprise data. Mastering asynchronous Python, data validation, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) prepares you to deliver reliable, production-grade AI solutions for scaling tech businesses.

column8: RAG Systems, Vector Databases, and Embeddings

column7: Static chatbots are obsolete; autonomous AI workers are the industry standard. You will learn to design intelligent, multi-agent swarms using frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Microsoft Autogen that can reason, plan, use web tools, and independently execute complex workflows.

Real-world business data is messy and diverse. This module equips you to process complex financial PDFs, vision, and real-time audio. By mastering advanced techniques like GraphRAG and algorithmic prompt optimization with DSPy, you can build sophisticated, multimodal AI architectures that solve complex operational challenges.

column8: Multimodal AI (Vision with GPT-4V/LLaVA, Audio with Whisper)

column7: Scaling AI securely and cost-effectively is the ultimate engineering hurdle. You will learn to fine-tune open-source models using LoRA to cut dependency on expensive APIs, serve them efficiently with vLLM, and deploy them on Kubernetes with strict enterprise security guardrails.

column8: Serving LLMs (vLLM, TGI, Quantization) and Token Economics, LLMOPS

column2: Advanced ML, Advanced Deep Learning, Advanced NLP
column5: Elective (Learners will have the option to continue in the course. If they wish to gain deeper understanding, they can choose to pursue further.)
column6: Advanced Machine Learning and Deep Learning 1
column7: A deep mathematical grasp of probability, statistics, optimization, and ML theory lets you go beyond simply using tools—enabling you to debug, improve, and innovate models from first principles. By mastering the mathematical foundations of core algorithms and statistical learning theory, you’ll sharpen your ability to analyze model behavior, derive algorithms from scratch, and tackle complex ML problems—skills that are invaluable for research roles and success in advanced ML interviews.

column6: Advanced Machine Learning and Deep Learning 2
column7: Diving deep into the mathematics and inner workings of neural networks—from backpropagation to interpretability—equips you to debug, fine-tune, and trust deep learning models at a fundamental level. You’ll also explore the theory behind transformers and attention mechanisms, the backbone of modern NLP models like BERT and GPT, enabling you to build, optimize, and innovate state-of-the-art language systems for both real-world impact and research-grade applications.

Strong DSA skills are the foundation of efficient problem-solving and are critical for cracking technical interviews at top companies. This module builds your algorithmic thinking and coding proficiency—enabling you to design optimal solutions across a wide range of real-world and interview problems.

column8: Array Problem Solving Patterns (Prefix, Sliding Windows, Subarrays, Subsets, Subsequences)
String Problem Solving Patterns, String Pattern Matching

column2: Advanced AI Engineering by CEC, IIT Roorkee

column7: Learn how to process and analyze massive datasets using Hadoop and Apache Spark. Master distributed data processing, real-time streaming, machine learning on Big Data, and data visualization with industry tools like Hive, Kafka, Tableau, and AWS QuickSight. Build practical skills to become a proficient Data Engineer.
column8:
- Hadoop & HDFS
- Spark (RDDs, DataFrames, Spark SQL)
- Spark Streaming & MLlib
- Hive & Kafka Integration
- Data Visualization (Tableau, AWS QuickSight)

column7: Master the fundamentals and advanced techniques of Reinforcement Learning, from solving MDPs with Dynamic Programming to model-free methods like Q-Learning and Policy Gradients. Explore state-of-the-art algorithms (PPO, SAC, TD3), model-based approaches, imitation learning, and real-world applications in multi-agent systems and robotics. Build practical skills to develop intelligent agents.
column8:
- Markov Decision Processes (MDPs)
- Dynamic Programming (Value & Policy Iteration)
- Q-Learning & DQN
- Policy Gradients & Actor-Critic
- PPO, SAC, TD3
- Imitation Learning & Multi-Agent RL

column7: Most professionals spend a significant portion of their day on tasks that are repetitive, formulaic, or simply time-consuming — writing status updates, summarising meeting notes, researching before a call. This module helps you reclaim that time. You will learn how to set up AI tools that genuinely understand your role and context, so that the mundane parts of your job get handled faster and your energy goes toward work that actually requires your judgment.

column7: You do not need to understand the engineering behind AI to use it well — but you do need accurate mental models of what it can and cannot do. This module gives you exactly that. You will learn why AI sometimes gets things wrong, how to evaluate the tools flooding the market, and how to protect yourself and your organisation from the kinds of mistakes that tend to be costly and embarrassing in professional settings.
column8:
● AI Mental Models
• How LLMs work in plain English (3 analogies, zero math)
• Tokens, context windows and why they matter
• What AI genuinely can't do (counting, exact math, recent events without search)

● Tool Landscape

This module moves you from guesswork to method. You will learn a structured approach to crafting prompts that produce consistently high-quality outputs, and you will build a reusable library that compounds in value the longer you use it.

The result is writing that is both faster and better.

column7: Operationalizing large language models demands a structured foundation that goes beyond conventional MLOps. A rigorous understanding of data curation, preprocessing pipelines, and lifecycle management is essential for building LLM systems that are reproducible, auditable, and production-ready.

General-purpose models rarely meet domain-specific performance requirements out of the box. Proficiency in fine-tuning methodologies and scalable serving infrastructure enables practitioners to adapt foundational models efficiently and deploy them with the reliability that enterprise applications demand.

Deploying an LLM is the beginning of an operational commitment, not the end. Systematic monitoring for drift, latency, and output quality — coupled with disciplined cost governance — ensures that LLM-powered systems remain performant, reliable, and economically sustainable at scale.
